subject: Ecology
title: Beavers: Wetland Engineers
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Beavers: Wetland Engineers.

hook.text: Beavers build dams, create ponds, and manufacture wetlands from scratch—nature's civil engineers.

buildup.text: A single beaver dam can flood hectares of forest, creating wetland habitat for hundreds of species.

discovery.text: Beaver ponds raise water tables, filter sediment, and store water that sustains streams in drought.

twist.text: Beavers were nearly extinct in North America by 1900. Their wetlands vanished with them.

climax.text: Reintroducing beavers is now a wetland restoration strategy used across North America and Europe.

punchline.text: Bring back the beaver and the wetland follows.

quiz.question: How do beaver dams benefit stream health?
options: They store water, filter sediment, and raise water tables
They eliminate all fish from streams
